meta.name: Geopolitical Conflict
meta.description: Models and analysis of conflicts between nation-states and non-state actors, including military strategy, proxy warfare, and technological escalations.
invariants: All actors (state and non-state), their capabilities, declared interests, and relationships MUST be explicitly defined and versioned.
Every action, escalation, or de-escalation event MUST be causally linked and attributed to a specific actor's decision or a preceding event.
The boundary between modeled/simulated events and observed real-world events MUST be unambiguously enforced at all times.
Assumptions, intelligence gaps, and information sources (including their assessed reliability) MUST be explicitly declared and propagated through any analysis.
The strategic objectives and defined victory/loss conditions for each primary actor MUST be explicitly stated.
Chains of command, control, and influence, including proxy relationships, MUST be explicitly mapped and auditable.
The potential for and consequences of technological escalation MUST be an explicit component of any strategic model.
